text: When you write code, bugs are almost inevitable. Finding them can be frustrating, but a systematic approach helps. Start by identifying the bug's symptoms - does the program crash, give wrong output, or freeze? Next, use debugging tools like breakpoints in your IDE to pause execution and inspect variable values. Another powerful strategy is logging: add print statements or use a logging library to track how data changes over time. This gives you a clear timeline of events. Instead of guessing, change one thing at a time and test. By being methodical, you can solve problems faster and learn more about your code.
